question_text: A motor has an efficiency of 80%. If 500J of electrical energy is supplied to the motor, how much useful mechanical energy does it produce?
correct_answer: Useful energy = 400J. This is calculated as: efficiency × total energy input = 0.80 × 500J = 400J
explanation: If a motor is 80% efficient, it means 80% of the input energy becomes useful work (mechanical energy), while 20% is wasted as heat and friction. So 80% of 500J = 400J of useful mechanical energy.
